WARNING: These test points are for A2442 (820-02652) ONLY. For other boards, use that board's diagnostic files. Component designators (CR765, CZ4xx, CZ6xx, PPU8xx, etc.) are board-specific — the same rail appears at DIFFERENT components on other boards.
description: Decision trees for 20V DCPS on A2442. Normal boot current is HIGHER than A2681 Air (2-4A vs 1.5-2.5A) due to Pro/Max SoC power. Max variant draws more than Pro.

scenarios.meaning: Board draws nothing. CD3217 or charger circuit completely non-functional.

steps.if_wrong: Charger IC not converting. Check CHGR_AUX_OK, I2C to charger.

steps.if_present: PMU not loading → check U7700/U8100 connections

scenarios.meaning: Board partially active but well below normal 2-4A boot range. A shorted rail is clamping a regulator.

steps.action: Run full chain: PPBUS → 3V8 → 5V → MPMU → Viper → Monaco
steps.next: Identify which stage has low/missing output

steps.measure: Diode mode on PP3V8_AON_MPMU, PP3V8_AON_SPMU, PP3V8_AON_WLBT, PP3V8_ATC01_LDO, PP3V8_ATC2_LDO branches
steps.data_note: Saf A2442: 20V/0.223A → U5500 (CD chip under-board liquid)

steps.measure: Diode mode on Viper output (CZ610-CZ618) and Monaco output (CA1xx)
steps.if_wrong: VR output shorted. Thermal cam to find hot SoC pin or decoupling cap.

steps.measure: Check PPU815-PPU836 test points (page 131) for each MPMU output
steps.if_any_missing: Thermal cam that rail → replace hot component or U8100

scenarios.count_note: Below normal boot range for Pro/Max
scenarios.meaning: Board drawing boot-level current for an Air, but insufficient for Pro/Max. SoC partially powered but Viper or Monaco may not be starting.

steps.if_missing: Viper not starting. Check U9300, VIPER_ALERT_L. Check power stages (CZ619-CZ654 area). This is the most likely cause of stalled boot on Pro/Max at this current range.

steps.measure: Probe Monaco output at CA100 area (page 50)
steps.if_missing: Monaco not starting. MONACO_ALERT_L. Max variant: more phases → more failure points.

steps.measure: Check NAND power: PP0V9_NAND0, PP0V9_NAND1, PP1V2_NAND0, PP1V2_NAND1 (Ocarina areas pages 102/107)
Backlight on? Image on external HDMI?
steps.if_no_display: Display power sequencer (page 111) or Luxe backlight (page 112) fault

scenarios.meaning: NORMAL for M1 Pro (2-3.5A) and M1 Max (2.5-4.5A). If customer reports a fault, it's peripheral not power.

steps.measure: Display, keyboard, trackpad, speakers, USB-C ports, HDMI, SD card

sub_checks.check: Luxe backlight (page 112), display power sequencer (page 111), eDP connector (page 110)
UF400 (left rear), UF500 (left front), or UG400 (right). Test each.

scenarios.meaning: Excessive even for Max. Major short or GPU under extreme load.

steps.if_functional: May be charging deeply discharged battery. Monitor — should settle to 2-4A.

steps.if_found: Identify component. Common: U5700 (regulating into 3V8 short), Viper/Monaco (shorted SoC domain), SoC (dead — BER).

steps.measure: Diode mode: CR765 (PPBUS) → CZ400 (3V8) → MC600 (5V) → CZ610 (Viper) → CA100 (Monaco)
steps.process: Find shorted rail. Thermal cam that specific area.
